task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly handles an empty list as a special case, returning None.
output:
```python
def median(nums):
    if not nums:
        return None

    sorted_nums = sorted(nums)

    middle_index = len(sorted_nums) // 2
    is_odd = len(sorted_nums) % 2 == 1

    median_value = sorted_nums[middle_index] if is_odd else (sorted_nums[middle_index - 1] + sorted_nums[middle_index]) / 2
    return median_value
```
trace: I used the built-in Python function `sort()` to sort the input list of numbers. Then, I calculated the middle index of the sorted list by taking the floor division and checking if the length is odd or even using modulo operation (`%`). If the length is odd, I returned the value at the middle index; otherwise, I found the average of the two middle values. The edge case handled in this solution is when the input list is empty, in which case the function returns `None`.
